posts.title: The doors are open
posts.excerpt: The Jane Company exists now: a clean first site with room for blog posts, music, and fiction.
posts.body:
The Jane Company is open. For now, the rooms are simple: a front room, a library, a music room, and a desk. That is enough. A site should begin as a place where the work can actually arrive, not as a machine so complicated it prevents the first post from being written.

This entry is stored in content/posts.json, which means it can be edited from the admin page once the editor is connected.

posts.title: Story shelves prepared
posts.excerpt: The story archive is ready for serial worlds, chapters, scene fragments, and fiction notes.
posts.body:
The library page is prepared as an archive for stories, worlds, scenes, chapters, and notes. The placeholders can be replaced one by one as real material is published.

Later, each story can have its own page. For now, the shelf exists, and that matters.

posts.title: Music room prepared
posts.excerpt: The music page is ready for tracks, lyrics, rough cuts, release notes, and links.
posts.body:
The music room is ready for songs, demos, lyrics, cover art, release notes, and outside links. It does not need to be crowded yet. The point is to have a room waiting when the first track is ready to walk in.

A featured record can sit at the front. Drafts and rough cuts can stay on the shelf until they are ready.
